commit.message:
fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions (#323)

* fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions

CMake captures the directory COMPILE_OPTIONS into each target's property
at the point the target is created (add_library / add_executable).  The
sanitizer and coverage blocks were placed after all library and test
target definitions, so add_compile_options() / add_link_options() had no
effect on any of those targets.

Move all USE_ASAN, USE_UBSAN, USE_TSAN, and USE_COVERAGE blocks to
immediately after the compiler-warning options and before any target or
find_package() calls.  This ensures --coverage, -fsanitize=*, etc. are
applied to G4OCCT, G4OCCTFixtureSupport, all test executables, and the
standalone app.

Verified locally: after the fix the coverage report covers 1118/1366
lines (81.8%) and 112/116 functions (96.6%), up from the broken 4/7
(57.1%) that only reflected inline header methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/9d2bd679-329a-486a-bd85-1794cfcb694e

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix(tests): delete G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler in TearDown; suppress Geant4 vis leaks

The fix to CMakeLists.txt that moves sanitizer options before target
definitions now correctly instruments all test executables with ASAN/UBSAN.
Two classes of new LeakSanitizer failures are addressed here:

1. G4OCCTTestEnv::TearDown() leak (unit tests 1–92)
   SetUp() installs a heap-allocated G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler via
   G4StateManager::SetExceptionHandler(), which does not take ownership.
   TearDown() restored the previous handler but never deleted ours, leaking
   16 bytes per test run.  Fix: retrieve the current handler before restoring
   the previous one, then delete it.

2. Geant4 RayTracer / PhysicalVolumeModel / scene leaks (vis tests 138–180)
   Geant4's visualization infrastructure (G4RayTracerViewer, G4OutBitStream,
   G4PhysicalVolumeModel::CreateCurrentAttValues, G4Scene::AddWorldIfEmpty,
   G4VisCommandSceneAddVolume::SetNewValue, etc.) allocates objects that are
   intentionally not freed at process exit.  These are upstream Geant4 leaks,
   not G4OCCT bugs.  Add suppressions to .github/lsan.supp covering all
   stack frames seen in the CI run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Fidelity-inspired row design with accent bar and SemiBold typography

Designer spec (IMG_1174.jpeg reference):

Accent bar (left edge of each transaction row):
- 3dp wide, full row height via IntrinsicSize.Min + fillMaxHeight()
- Green (PositiveText) for +$ amounts, red (NegativeText) for -$ amounts
- AccentNeutral (#C4C9D4) for unknown/neutral rows
- Color signaled from amount Text to Row via LocalAccentColorSink SideEffect

Row layout:
- Outer Row with height(IntrinsicSize.Min) anchors accent bar height
- Content Row: start=12dp, end=16dp, top/bottom=13dp (→ 68dp row height)
- Left column wrapped in Box(weight(1f, fill=false)) — yields space to amount
- 8dp Spacer minimum gap guard between description and amount
- CenterVertically alignment for amount vs description+date column

Typography:
- Description text: SemiBold when LocalBodyEmphasis=true (inside tx rows)
- Amount text: SemiBold + TextAlign.End + semantic color
- Caption/date text: unchanged (muted gray, Normal weight)

Column override:
- Arrangement.spacedBy(2dp) for description→date gap
- No fillMaxWidth() — inner columns wrap content correctly

New token: AccentNeutral = Color(0xFFC4C9D4) in Color.kt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove fillMaxWidth from Column override — restores amount visibility

The financialColumnWidget was applying fillMaxWidth() to ALL Columns,
causing the left description Column inside SpaceBetween Rows to consume
the full row width and push amount text off-screen to the right.

Fix: remove fillMaxWidth() from the override. Outer card Column still
fills width naturally (widest child — the fillMaxWidth SpaceBetween Row —
forces it). Inner transaction Columns now wrap content, leaving room
for the right-side amount Text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: apply designer spacing specs to card rows and columns

Per Android Designer specs (Robinhood/Fidelity-standard):

Row overrides (spaceBetween rows):
- Vertical padding: 10dp → 12dp (yields 64dp canonical financial row height)
- Horizontal padding: +4dp per side (12dp card + 4dp row = 16dp MD3 content margin)
- verticalAlignment: CenterVertically (amount aligns to midpoint of desc+date)

Column override (all columns):
- Add Arrangement.spacedBy(2dp) — creates label→metadata visual hierarchy
  between description text and date text per MD3 two-line ListItem pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add row padding to transaction rows in FinancialCatalog

- Override Row widget with financialRowWidget in FinancialCatalog
- spaceBetween rows (transaction rows, header row) get 10dp vertical padding
  for breathing room and minimum touch target compliance
- verticalAlignment set to CenterVertically for spaceBetween rows so
  amount text aligns with the midpoint of the description+date column
- All other Row distributions use default Top alignment with no extra padding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reject TOML-invalid control characters in comments

validate_inline_comment() and build_block_comment() accepted control
characters (U+0000-U+0008, U+000A-U+001F, U+007F) which are forbidden
in TOML comments.  Setting such a comment produced invalid TOML that
could not be re-parsed.

fix: array insert no longer inherits block comments from existing elements

multiline_prefix() and save_first_prefix() were copying the first
element's entire decor prefix — including block comments — onto newly
inserted values.  This caused insert(0, x) and insert(n, x) to give
the new element a stale block comment that belonged to another element.

Improve tab completions grid rendering logic (#365)

Implements four improvements to the tab completion grid rendering:

## Changes Made

- **Early "No suggestions" exit**: When there are zero filtered
suggestions, the "No suggestions" message is printed immediately at the
top of the render block and the grid-building logic is skipped entirely.

- **Middle-ellipsis truncation for wide suggestions**: If a suggestion's
text is wider than the terminal width and it falls in the first visible
column, `render()` now applies middle truncation (e.g.
`very_long_path_name` → `very_l…ame`) instead of silently discarding the
column.

- **2D selected position**: `ActiveSuggestions` now tracks
`(selected_row, selected_col)` instead of a flat index. Up/down arrows
navigate within a column; left/right arrows jump between columns. If
navigating right would land on an empty cell in a partial last column,
the selection wraps back to column 0.

- **Column scroll offset**: A `col_scroll_offset` field ensures the
selected column is always visible. `into_grid()` accepts a `col_offset`
parameter to skip leading columns, and the offset is updated after each
render and eagerly on navigation so there is no visible lag.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Tab completions grid improvements:
> if the number of active suggestions is zero, the print the No
suggestions at the start of the render block and exit that block.
> 
> If the suggestion is too wide to fit onto a single line and we are
drawing the first column, the remove the middle part of the suggestion
and replace it with the ellipses character.
> 
> Keep track of the selected position as a 2d value, not just an index.
When you move to the right, increase the column index. The in the index
check function, if there isn't a suggestion for that 2d pos, set col to
0 etc.
> 
> If the cursor goes too far to the right, we currently wont be able to
draw this column. So we should skip some columns so that the column with
the cursor is always drawn.


</details>

* feat: Improve clearance status tracking and unify payment logic

This commit introduces significant improvements to how student clearance statuses are tracked and managed, alongside a major refactoring to unify the payment handling logic for both fees and fines.

Key Changes:
- **Clearance Status Improvements**:
  - Restructured the `blockingItems` within the `clearanceStatus` document to track individual fine items (using a unique reference ID) rather than just the aggregate fine document. This allows for more granular tracking and visualization of what exactly is blocking a student's clearance.
  - Updated `generateFinesOnEvent` to update the student's clearance document with the specific fine item ID, amount, and status ("unpaid") when a new fine is generated.
  - Refined `fetchClearanceDocuments` and related utilities to handle this new granular structure.
- **Unified Payment Backend**:
  - Centralized the backend payment history logic in `paymentHistory.ts`. Functions like `addOfflineFinesPayment` were renamed and refactored to generic `addOfflinePayment` functions that support both `FEES` and `FINES` contexts.
  - Updated `clearance.ts` to use these centralized verification and rejection functions, ensuring consistent behavior when acknowledging or rejecting payments from the clearance management interface.
  - Refactored `fees.ts` to remove redundant payment transaction functions in favor of the unified backend.
- **UI Enhancements**:
  - Reorganized the Payments and Clearance interfaces.
  - Added new components: `ClearanceCard`, `ClearanceTable`, and `LogManualPaymentDialog` for better data presentation.
  - Split the Payments page into modular `SubmissionsTab` and `UnpaidTab` components, with corresponding Card and Table views for each.
- **TypeScript & Type Definitions**: Updated `PaymentType` and `ClearanceStatus` related interfaces in `types.ts` to reflect the new data structures.

These changes ensure that the clearance system accurately reflects real-time financial obligations and simplifies the codebase by reusing the same robust payment processing logic for all transaction types.

* fix(payment): correctly attribute payment history in bulk transactions and remove redundant clearance initialization

    - **Bulk Payment History Refactoring**: Modified `recordBulkManualPaymentAndUpdateClearance` in `fees.ts` to ensure that when a bulk payment is processed, the `paymentHistory` log is created under the specific subcollection of each individual fee or fine item being paid, rather than generic root-level payment history.

This redundant step could potentially cause state conflicts, as actual clearance updates are handled properly upon specific fine interactions."

* style(clearance): enhance UI for manual payment logging dialog

This commit improves the user interface of the `LogManualPaymentDialog` by organizing unsettled items into distinct, intuitive groups (Fees, Fines, Other Requirements) and enhancing the visual hierarchy of the selection process.

Key Changes:
- **Grouped Items**: Unsettled items are now grouped by their type (`fees`, `fines`, `other`), making it easier for admins to identify what the student is paying for.
- **Improved List Items**:
  - Added specific icons (`CreditCard` for fees, `AlertCircle` for fines) to distinguish item types visually.
  - Enhanced the styling of individual selectable items, including dynamic border and background colors based on the item type (e.g., green accents for fees, red accents for fines).
- **Sub-Totals Breakdown**: The selection summary now displays a detailed breakdown of the total amount selected, showing separate sub-totals for Fees, Fines, and Other items, giving admins a clearer picture of the transaction composition before logging it.
- **Styling Tweaks**: Removed obsolete borders and utilized Tailwind's `cn` utility for cleaner class merging and state-based styling.

commit.message:
Fix slice assignment invalidation precision

Same-length slice replacement now stamps only the replaced indices
instead of everything from the first replaced index to the end of the
array.  Different-length replacement (which shifts elements) still
bumps from the first affected index onward.

Also fixes a pre-existing bug where pure insertion via empty slice
(e.g. arr[2:2] = [9]) failed to invalidate shifted proxies.

feat: implement A2UI v0.8 spec-compliant improvements

- Path-based DataModel bindings: server transforms literalString → path
  references, data separated into dataModelUpdate operations
- Chunked surfaceUpdate: components split into batches of 15 for
  progressive streaming
- Progressive rendering: client updates UI incrementally as each
  A2UI operation arrives instead of waiting for Done
- Event round-trip: /event endpoint processes userAction events,
  sends to LLM, streams back new A2UI operations via SSE
- Operation reordering: text → begin → data → surface chunks → done
  ensures paths resolve immediately when components render
- Interactive widgets: added TextField, CheckBox, List to system prompt
- Loading indicator: LinearProgressIndicator on A2UI surface during stream

Fix KeysView.__or__/__xor__ to accept any iterable

Python's dict_keys set operations accept any iterable, not just sets.
Our __or__ and __xor__ delegated to set.__or__/set.__xor__ which
reject non-set operands like lists and tuples.

Fix by converting the other operand to a set first via iterable_to_pyset
helper before calling the set method.
